_comment: Detective-audit overrides for the Shipwright FRAMEWORK monorepo. Shallow-merged over the shipwright-compliance audit defaults by load_audit_config. disabled_checks lists detective checks that are STRUCTURALLY not-applicable to this adopted, multi-component, active-CI framework repo (each rewritten to SKIP, not FAIL). A single FR-driven consumer app keeps all checks on (empty default).

_disabled_checks_reasons.A5.6: Security CI workflow is intentionally ACTIVE (pull_request/schedule) here, not dormant — the dormant-trigger contract targets staged-but-inactive projects.
_disabled_checks_reasons.B7: Adopted multi-component framework monorepo: most commits are plugin development / merges / chores, not iterate work_completed events — 'every commit since release has a matching event' does not apply.
_disabled_checks_reasons.G2: Monorepo uses multi-component conventional-commit scopes (e.g. 'canon', 'build,iterate', 'hooks') that are not split-names in the plan alias-map.

_d4_reenabled: D4 was disabled because it 'can only ever surface STALE historical snapshots' — a work_completed gap (passed<total) is host-gated SKIPS on a green-at-merge build, not a failing build, so D4 keyed on the raw gap false-flagged (e.g. FR-01.07 4955/4967, long green). iterate-2026-07-23-tests-skipped-tracking fixed the ROOT cause: D4 now keys on genuine failures (total-passed-skipped), treating a skip-count-less gap charitably as skips. D4 passes honestly on this repo, so the suppression is removed — a FUTURE explicit-skip residual (a real Iron-Law violation) now surfaces instead of being hidden.
_d1_reenabled: D1 was disabled while FR-tagging was frozen (no-FR tooling iterates left FRs un-event-tracked). BP-1 re-enables it: D1 now uses all-time coverage (no spec-update watermark) and every spec FR is covered by an event, so D1 passes honestly instead of being suppressed.
